title: Solomon anointed amid succession crisis
summary_text: As David grows old, Adonijah tries to seize the throne, but Nathan, Bathsheba, Zadok, and Benaiah move quickly to secure Solomon's anointing. David publicly confirms Solomon as his chosen successor, and the people celebrate the new king. The chapter closes with Adonijah seeking mercy as Solomon begins his reign with legitimacy and peace.
key_points: Adonijah attempts to claim the throne without David's approval.
Nathan and Bathsheba appeal to David to act before the crisis grows.
Solomon is anointed king and proclaimed by the people.
Adonijah's bid fails, and he seeks refuge at the altar.

title: David's charge and Solomon's first judgments
summary_text: David gives Solomon final instructions to walk faithfully, guard the kingdom, and deal wisely with unresolved matters in the court. Solomon secures his throne by removing threats from Adonijah, Abiathar, Joab, and Shimei. The chapter shows that the new king's rule must be rooted in obedience, justice, and discernment.
key_points: David charges Solomon to keep the LORD's commandments.
Solomon acts decisively to secure the throne.
Adonijah's request becomes another threat to the kingdom.
Shimei's disobedience brings the sentence David had warned about.
evidence_phrases: be strong, and show yourself a man
keep the charge of the LORD

title: Solomon asks for wisdom
summary_text: At Gibeon, Solomon asks God for a listening heart rather than riches, long life, or victory over enemies. God grants him unmatched wisdom along with wealth and honor because he asked for discernment to govern well. His first judgment between two women proves that the gift is real and that the people can trust his rule.
key_points: Solomon asks for an understanding heart to judge the people.
God grants wisdom, riches, and honor beyond all kings.
The dispute over the child reveals Solomon's discernment.
Israel sees that the king's wisdom is from God.
evidence_phrases: I have given you a wise and understanding heart
there was no one like you

title: Prosperity, administration, and wisdom
summary_text: Solomon's kingdom is organized through a wide network of officials, districts, and provisions that keep the royal court well supplied. Peace extends throughout the land, and the king's wisdom surpasses that of the surrounding sages. His rule is marked by order, abundance, and a reputation that draws admiration from every direction.
key_points: Solomon appoints officials over the kingdom's needs.
Each district provides food for the royal household.
The land enjoys peace under Solomon's rule.
His wisdom exceeds the wisdom of others.
evidence_phrases: he was wiser than all men

title: Temple preparations with Hiram
summary_text: Solomon secures peace with Hiram of Tyre and begins gathering cedar, labor, and supplies for the temple. Israel and Lebanon cooperate so that a house for the LORD can be built without war or distraction. The chapter emphasizes orderly preparation and international partnership under God's blessing.
key_points: Hiram supplies cedar and skilled labor.
Solomon organizes a large workforce for the project.
The LORD gives Solomon rest on every side.
Materials are gathered for a worthy house for God.
evidence_phrases: for the house of the LORD

title: The temple takes shape
summary_text: Solomon begins building the temple in careful obedience to the pattern he has received. The measurements, chambers, and inner structure stress holiness, precision, and permanence. The chapter presents the house of God as a place built with reverence and order from the foundation upward.
key_points: The temple is built according to exact measurements.
The inner sanctuary is set apart as the holiest place.
Stones are prepared quietly before they are set in place.
The house is shaped for God's dwelling among his people.
evidence_phrases: the house, when it was in building

title: Palace work and temple furnishings
summary_text: Solomon spends years building his own palace complex and then details the bronze artistry that equips the temple. Hiram's work produces pillars, the bronze sea, stands, and vessels that make worship possible. The chapter highlights both royal magnificence and the careful furnishing of the sacred house.
key_points: Solomon's palace complex takes much longer to describe than the temple furnishings.
Hiram fashions the bronze work for sacred use.
The temple is supplied with stands, basins, and vessels.
The chapter closes with a finished inventory of holy objects.

title: Ark brought in and temple dedicated
summary_text: The ark is brought into the completed temple with sacrifice, singing, and a solemn procession of priests. Solomon then prays that God will hear from heaven in every kind of distress and forgive his people when they turn toward this house. The chapter climaxes with the glory of the LORD filling the temple and confirming his presence among Israel.
key_points: The ark is placed in the most holy place.
Solomon blesses the assembly and recalls God's faithfulness.
He prays that God will hear from heaven and forgive.
The glory of the LORD fills the house.

title: God's warning and Solomon's building works
summary_text: The LORD appears to Solomon again, confirming blessing for obedience and warning of judgment if the king or his descendants turn aside. Solomon's other building projects, trade routes, and labor systems show the scale of his power. The chapter balances privilege with warning, reminding Israel that success cannot replace covenant faithfulness.
key_points: God again appears to Solomon and speaks plainly about obedience.
Solomon completes major building projects.
Trade and labor extend his influence widely.
The chapter warns that disobedience would bring ruin.
evidence_phrases: if you shall walk before me
this house, which I have consecrated

title: The queen of Sheba visits Solomon
summary_text: The queen of Sheba comes to test Solomon with hard questions and leaves convinced that his wisdom and prosperity exceed every report. She praises the God who has set him on the throne and observes the order and abundance of his court. The chapter celebrates Solomon's international fame while also showing the splendor of his kingdom.
key_points: The queen comes with difficult questions.
Solomon answers all her questions.
She recognizes that God has delighted in Solomon.
The chapter records the wealth and grandeur of his reign.
evidence_phrases: there was no more spirit in her
all the earth sought the presence of Solomon
the king made silver to be in Jerusalem

title: Solomon's heart turns after other gods
summary_text: Solomon's foreign marriages lead his heart away from exclusive loyalty to the LORD, and he begins to serve the gods of his wives. Because of this compromise, God raises adversaries against him and announces that the kingdom will be torn from his house. The chapter shows how a gifted king can still fall when covenant loyalty is neglected.
key_points: Solomon's many wives turn his heart after other gods.
The LORD announces judgment on the kingdom.
Adversaries rise up against Solomon in his later years.
Jeroboam is marked out as a future ruler over part of Israel.
evidence_phrases: his wives turned away his heart
the LORD was angry with Solomon
I will surely tear the kingdom

title: The kingdom divides under Rehoboam
summary_text: Rehoboam rejects the elders' counsel and answers the people with harsher demands than his father had imposed. The northern tribes rebel, crowning Jeroboam and breaking the united kingdom in two. The chapter marks the turning point from Solomon's glory to long division between Israel and Judah.
key_points: Rehoboam refuses wise counsel from the elders.
The people reject the house of David's harsh reply.
Jeroboam becomes king over the northern tribes.
The kingdom is permanently divided.
evidence_phrases: what portion have we in David?

title: A prophet confronts Bethel's altar
summary_text: A man of God from Judah confronts Jeroboam's altar at Bethel and announces that idolatrous worship will be judged. The sign of the broken altar and the withered hand confirms that God's word stands above royal religion. Jeroboam's stubbornness begins a pattern of resistance that will haunt the northern kingdom.
key_points: The man of God condemns the altar at Bethel.
A sign confirms the truth of the prophecy.
Jeroboam's hand is restored only after appeal.
Disobedience ends in death for the man of God.

title: Judgment on Jeroboam's house
summary_text: Jeroboam sends his wife to Ahijah, but the prophet announces that their dynasty will be cut off because Jeroboam led Israel into sin. In Judah, Rehoboam's reign is also marked by evil and spiritual decline. The chapter makes clear that both kingdoms are already bearing the cost of unfaithfulness.
key_points: Ahijah announces the fall of Jeroboam's house.
Jeroboam's son Abijah dies as foretold.
Rehoboam also leads Judah into sin.
Both kingdoms show the fruit of covenant rebellion.
evidence_phrases: there was found in him some good thing

title: Asa reforms Judah
summary_text: Abijam follows the pattern of his father, but Asa brings a measure of reform by removing idols and strengthening Judah. He seeks the LORD in his trouble and is given victory and stability. The chapter contrasts fragile monarchy with the blessing that comes from covenant renewal.
key_points: Abijam continues the sins of his father.
Asa removes idols and seeks the LORD.
Judah and Israel remain in conflict.
God's help strengthens Asa's kingdom.
evidence_phrases: did evil in the sight of the LORD

title: Israel's unstable kings
summary_text: Baasha, Elah, Zimri, Omri, and Ahab rise and fall in quick succession, showing the instability of the northern throne. The LORD's word against Baasha is fulfilled, and Ahab's reign introduces even deeper idolatry. Political success cannot hide the spiritual collapse that is taking hold in Israel.
key_points: Baasha's house is judged for sin.
Zimri's brief reign ends in self-destruction.
Omri stabilizes the throne through force.
Ahab becomes king and worsens Israel's idolatry.
evidence_phrases: did evil in the sight of the LORD

title: Elijah fed by ravens and widow's oil
summary_text: Elijah suddenly appears during a drought and is sustained first by ravens and then by a widow in Zarephath whose flour and oil do not fail. When her son dies, Elijah prays and God restores the child to life. The chapter proves that the LORD can preserve life and provide for his servant even in famine and death.
key_points: Elijah is sustained by ravens at the brook Cherith.
A widow in Zarephath feeds Elijah by God's word.
Her flour and oil are miraculously preserved.
The child's life is restored through Elijah's prayer.
evidence_phrases: the jar of meal did not waste

title: Fire on Carmel and rain returns
summary_text: Elijah confronts Ahab, gathers Israel at Carmel, and exposes the futility of Baal worship. The LORD answers Elijah with fire, and then the prophet prays until the long drought breaks in a rushing rainstorm. The chapter shows that covenant loyalty belongs to the LORD alone and that he alone brings life to a dry land.
key_points: Elijah calls Israel to choose between the LORD and Baal.
Baal does not answer, but the LORD sends fire.
The people confess that the LORD is God.
Rain finally returns after Elijah's prayer.
evidence_phrases: there is the sound of abundance of rain

title: Elijah at Horeb
summary_text: After Jezebel's threat, Elijah flees in exhaustion and despair, but God meets him in wilderness provision and quiet speech. At Horeb the LORD recommissions Elijah and reveals that he is not alone, preserving a faithful remnant and appointing future leaders. The chapter moves from fear to renewed purpose under God's gentle yet sovereign care.
key_points: Elijah collapses under fear and fatigue.
God strengthens him for the journey to Horeb.
The LORD speaks in a gentle voice, not in the storm.
Elijah is sent back with a new commission.
evidence_phrases: What are you doing here, Elijah?

title: Ahab and Ben-hadad
summary_text: Ben-hadad attacks Israel, but God delivers the Syrians into Ahab's hand twice, showing mercy to a flawed king. Ahab then spares Ben-hadad contrary to God's word, and a prophet announces judgment for that disobedience. The chapter warns that victory means little when obedience is neglected.
key_points: The LORD gives victory over a superior enemy.
Ahab spares the enemy king against God's command.
The prophet exposes the king's disobedience.
Ahab is told that his life will answer for his decision.
evidence_phrases: I will deliver it into your hand
the LORD is a God of the hills
your life shall go for his life

title: Naboth's vineyard and Ahab's guilt
summary_text: Ahab covets Naboth's vineyard, and Jezebel arranges Naboth's murder so the king can seize it. Elijah confronts Ahab with a sentence of doom on both the king and his house, yet Ahab humbles himself enough to delay immediate disaster. The chapter exposes how greed and abuse of power invite the judgment of God.
key_points: Ahab wants Naboth's vineyard for himself.
Jezebel engineers Naboth's death.
Elijah announces judgment on Ahab's house.
Ahab's humility delays the sentence temporarily.
evidence_phrases: You have killed and also taken possession

title: Micaiah's warning and Ahab's death
summary_text: Jehoshaphat joins Ahab in battle, but Micaiah alone speaks the truth that the campaign will fail. Ahab disguises himself, yet a random arrow fulfills God's word and the king dies in the fight. The closing summary leaves Israel and Judah still entangled in compromise, but God's word has not failed.
key_points: Micaiah warns that defeat is certain.
Ahab ignores the prophecy and goes to battle.
An arrow strikes Ahab despite his disguise.
The king dies exactly as God had said.
evidence_phrases: a certain man drew his bow
